titleStmt.title: SII 17.49: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 49. (A.R. No. 47 of 1904). KUḌATINI, BELLARY TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a fragment of slab built into the platform in front of the Kumārasvāmin temple. Nityavarsha. (India III).
This inscription engraved in early Nāgarī characters consists of three sections (A.B and C) of which the third (C) is engraved in a different hand and does not appear to belong to the first two.

Section A belongs to the Rāshṭrakūṭa king Nityavarshadēva Pṛithvī-vallabha who is stated to have meditated at the feet of Akālavarshadēva. The Śaka year cited at the beginning is lost due to damage and Vikrama, Māgha, śu. Rathasaptamī, Sunday, are the only details of date available (See Introduction)••••
Section B seems to deal with the exploits of a chief called Aiyaṇaiya and refers to a princess of the Sindu (for Sindhu ?) family. The date is lost here also.

Section C mentions Dhōra and describes the achievements of the two chieftains belonging to the Chālukya family, Balavarman and his son, Daśavarman. Kāñchi and the planting of the two teeth of a tusker are mentioned in this connection.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
ṣvaṅkataḥ saṁva
pravarddhamānavikramasaṁvatsarānta
rgatamāghamāsaśuklarathasaptamyāṁ(myām)The letters given in the round brackets in this text sus�t corrections.
Ādityavāre ramāsyāṁRead �yāṁsaṁvatsaramāsapa
kṣadivasapūrvvāyāṁ tithau paramabhaṭṭāra
ka mahārājādhirāja paramaśvara śrī
madakālavarṣadevapādānudhyāta paramabha
ṭṭārakamahārājādhirāja parameśvara śrī
mannityavarṣadevapṛthvīvallabhaśrīva
dopajīvī
rendraputrā

māsa ga
. papā devī pra
ṅki sarvvānadhi ko bhūdindra
dhikobhūdaiyaṇaiyanṛpatirayaṁ saḥ
durgamagrahītsamare nirjitya ripurājendrān .
ndrendrabhūvalayavallabhapādapadmabhaktibhramadbhramara Eṣa tathāpya
leṣu dhavalaḥ prabhuraiyaṇaiyaḥ . sau(śau)rye harissakalapūrṇṇa
rttasvāmī viśrutakīrttirūrjitaguṇastīrtheṣva
vijṛmbhitena mahatā kenāpi luptodayaḥ soyaṁ śrībharadaṁkakāra
pratiṣṭhāṁ punaḥ . prāyaḥ svāmihitaikavṛttiradhikaśrīmā
ṇaḥ . śrīmatsindu(ndhu)kulānvayaikatilakā tyāhi śailābhibakā .
bhidhānanṛpaterlakṣmīrivāṁbhonidheḥ . tasyā ḥ śrīghaṭemalla
raiyaṇaiyaḥ prabhuḥ . svāmī prastutakārttikeyamakaro
sthāyinaṁ(nam) . tha . śakanṛpakālātītasaṁvatsaraśate

śrīmāṁścālū(lu)kyavaṁśe samajani nihatāśeṣaśatru
kṣitīśo nyāyāpeta(tāṁ) sa pṛthvī(thvīṁ) caturudadhilatāveṣṭi
tāṁ yaḥ prabhūtāt . Ākṛṣyādātukāmo nṛpasadasi ta
dā dhoranāmne mahomna(ndro) .The daṇḍa is unnecessary.dūṣyodvaddaṇḍikāyāṁ sanakṛta bala
varmo(rmā)dhipaḥ paṭṭabandhaṁ(ndhan) . saṁprāmaikarasaḥ parākramadhanaḥ kāṁcī
sa(śa)mugrāhave jitvā so(vo)ragajendradantayugalaṁ saṁsyāpya nāmāṁkitaṁ(tam) . khyā
tālaṁpū(pu)ranāmadheyanagarabrahmeśvarasyāgrataḥ .The daṇḍa is unnecessary.soyaṁ tasya sutobhya
dhāyi daśavarmākhyaḥ pracaṇḍodayaḥ . tasya sūturabhavatparākramākrāntavi
śvaripubhūpamaṇḍalaḥ . Anvayaikatilakaḥ
Iti
Digital edition of SII 17.49 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
